title: My life as a Russian exile in Paris: 'You can't fight French bureaucracy'
subhead: 'A Russian in Paris' (1/6). Journalist Elizaveta Osetinskaya, who came to France in 2022 because of her opposition to the war in Ukraine, describes the difficulties she has faced with the prefecture while barely speaking the language.

Widespread drying of soil and rivers in France raises alarm among hydrologists
subhead: Surface water and soil have been gripped by severe drought. While the situation for groundwater might be different, tensions are simmering over how water is being used.

title: ''No kids' policies promote a polarized and reductive vision of society'
subhead: As child-free spaces continue to grow rapidly, a group of non-profit leaders, researchers and artists argue for Le Monde that the youngest members of society should not be seen as a nuisance to be tolerated, but as a valuable presence to be welcomed.

title: Fire ravages forest in southwestern France as thousands evacuated
subhead: Thousands have been evacuated from a town in Gironde in southwestern France after a fast-moving fire broke out on Wednesday.

title: How Bernard Arnault works to keep his media outlets in line
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (4/6). Since the 1990s, the CEO of LVMH has built a small media galaxy in print and radio, which he uses to convey his ideas and tastes, but also to promote his brands.

How social media bans for young users, now in effect in France, have spread worldwide
subhead: France has decided to ban those under 15 from major platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. The country is following a global trend that has gained momentum in recent years.

title: Climate scientists aren't surprised by summer's record heat: 'We've entered a dangerous zone'
subhead: Record heat, historic drought and sweeping wildfires have marked France's 2026 summer. Yet climate scientists mostly see global warming's expected effects playing out. Only the rapid succession of heatwaves has caught them off guard.

title: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.
